INTERSECTION OF FRAZEE ROAD AND MURRAY CANYON ROAD IN MISSION VALLEY FLOODED. WATER LEVEL WAS UP TO THE TOP OF THE TIRES OF SEVERAL CARS IN A PARKING LOT ON THE SOUTHWEST CORNER OF THE INTERSECTION.

FLOODWATERS ROSE TO THE CEILING OF SEVERAL APARTMENTS AND HOMES BUILT BELOW GRADE ON 48TH STREET IN CITY HEIGHTS. RESIDENTS HAD TO EVACUATE QUICKLY IN ORDER TO ESCAPE INJURY. ONE DOG PERISHED. ABOUT 10-12 PEOPLE HAVE BEEN DISPLACED FROM THEIR RESIDENCES.

HEAVY BOULDERS...WEIGHING APPROXIMATELY 1000 POUNDS...WERE BROUGHT DOWN ONTO OLD CASTLE ROAD IN NEAR VALLEY CENTER...BLOCKING BOTH LANES.

ORANGE COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS REPORTS 3 MODERATE DEBRIS FLOWS IN THE SILVERADO CANYON AREA AT AROUND 1030 PM WED JAN 6. THE FIRST WAS 40-50 CUBIC YARDS LONG AND OCCURRED AT SILVERADO CANYON ROAD AND HAFRA WAY BLOCKING THE ROAD. THE SECOND WAS AT THE K-RAIL 100-200 FEET PAST SMEISIC RANCH. 1-2 FEET DEEP AND 10 CUBIC YARDS LONG AND BLOCKED SILVERADO CANYON ROAD AT THAT LOCATION. THE THIRD WAS AT THE SMEISIC RANCH RAVINE ABOUT 20-30 CUBIC YARDS ACROSS BUT DID NOT CROSS THE ROAD. THE ROAD WAS CLEARED AND RE-OPENED AT 1 AM.
6 INCHES OF MUD ACROSS ALL LANES AT DEHESA ROAD NEAR STALLION OAKS ROAD.
UPDATE TO PREVIOUS REPORT: MUDSLIDE IN THE RANCHO SAN DIEGO AREA OF EL CAJON ON WILLOW GLEN SENT MUD FLOWING INTO SEVERAL HOMES AND CLOSED DEHESA ELEMENTARY SCHOOL FOR AT LEAST 2 DAYS.

5 POWER POLES DOWNED BY WIND AND UP TO 7 DAMAGED AND REPORTED TO RIVERSIDE COUNTY EOC. EDOM HILL HAD GUST 79 MPH BETWEEN 5 AND 6 PM FRIDAY. APPEARED RELATED TO THE 10 POLES REPORTED DAMAGED UPSTREAM IN RIVERSIDE AT 330 PM.

*** 1 INJ *** CHP REPORTS LOW VISIBILITY CAUSED TRAFFIC ACCIDENT INVOLVING 15 VEHICLES, INCLUDING TWO SEMI'S, ON HIGHWAY 247 JUST NORTH OF RABBIT SPRINGS ROAD. ONE INJURY. ROAD SHUT DOWN.

*** 25 INJ *** CHP REPORTS LOW VISIBILITY CAUSED TRAFFIC ACCIDENT INVOLVING 15 VEHICLES, INCLUDING TWO SEMI'S, ON HIGHWAY 247 JUST NORTH OF RABBIT SPRINGS ROAD. 20-25 MINOR INJURIES, ONE MAJOR INJURY. ROAD SHUT DOWN.
LIFEGUARDS REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 1 MILE OFFSHORE OF BLACKS BEACH AT 5:35 PM MOVING EAST AT 40 KNOTS. IT MADE LANDFALL AS A WEAK TORNADO AT 5:38 PM JUST NORTH OF THE GLIDER PORT AND DISSIPATED.
LIFEGUARDS REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 1 MILE OFFSHORE OF BLACKS BEACH AT 5:35 PM MOVING EAST AT 40 KNOTS. IT MADE LANDFALL AT 5:38 PM JUST NORTH OF THE GLIDER PORT AND DISSIPATED.
CHP REPORTED FLOODING ON HWY 395 NEAR SHADOW MOUNTAIN ROAD. VEHICLES ARE SPINNING OUT BECAUSE OF THE WATER ON THE ROADWAY.
3 FT OF WATER IN RIGHT LANE ALONG INTERSTATE 8, AT WARING RD. IN THE AREA OF A PREVIOUS SINKHOLE. ROCKS AND DEBRIS ON ROAD AND VEHICLES SLIDING AROUND.

DELAYED REPORT. USACE AND BUREAU OF INDIAN AFFAIRS REPORTED A SEVERELY DAMAGE ROADWAY THAT LEADS INTO THE LOS COYOTES RESERVATION. THE ROAD IS DOWN TO ONE LANE BUT IS UNSTABLE AND HAS CULVERT DAMAGE. DEBRIS FLOW WAS ALSO REPORTED. USACE ENGINEERS VISITED THE LOCATION ON WEDNESDAY. THE ROADWAY WILL NEED EXTENSIVE REPAIRS. MULTI AGENCY CONFERENCE CALLS ARE IN PROCESS.
CHP REPORTS FLOODING IN THE HOV LANE ON SR-60 EAST NEAR VINEYARD AVENUE.
CARS PARTIALLY SUBMERGED NEAR THE ONTARIO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT AT GROVE AVENUE AND FRANCIS STREET...RESULTING IN THE INTERSECTION BEING TEMPORARILY SHUT DOWN. EXACT TIMING OF FLOOD IS UNCERTAIN.
CHP REPORT OF VEHICLES UNABLE TO MOVE DUE TO WATER ON THE ROAD AT THE SR-60 AND VINEYARD AVENUE ON-RAMP.
WINDS AVERAGING SW AT 39 MPH WITH GUSTS TO 50 MPH. BLOWING DUST WITH VISIBILITY BETWEEN A 1/4 TO 1/8 MILE.
FLASH FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 66 AT BRYMAN ROAD SOUTH OF HELENDALE. COUNTY HAS SHUT DOWN THE ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 66 AT TURNER ROAD.
SPOTTER REPORTS 6 INCHES OF WATER ABOUT 50 FEET WIDE FLOWING DOWN BUENA VISTA ROAD. THERE'S ALSO DIRT AND ROCK ON THE ROADS, INCLUDING HIGHWAY 18 AND CUSTER ROAD. 1.5 INCHES OF TOTAL RAIN REPORTED. .
DEBRIS (MUD/DIRT/ROCKS) ON ROADWAY. PLOW IS NEEDED TO CLEAR LANES.
